channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 2 transgender women stabbed to death at home in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two men armed with daggers killed two transgender women at their home in conservative northwest Pakistan overnight before fleeing the scene, police said, a sign of increasing violence against trans people in the country.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny's posthumous memoir is a testament to resilience
item.description: In his posthumous memoir “Patriot,” Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny tracks the boredom, isolation, exhaustion, suffering and absurdity of prison life, while commenting on everything from French literature to Billie Eilish.
item.dc:creator: Hillel Italie And Dasha Litvinova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says only a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway a reluctant Germany
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his so-called “victory plan” after the U.S. election, but concedes that its key demand — an invitation to join NATO — is not welcome by some major Western partners, in particular Germany.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Son of Singapore's founder says he has been granted political asylum in the UK
item.description: Lee Hsien Yang, the estranged brother of former Singapore Prime Minister Lee Hsien Loong, says he is now a “political refugee” after the British government granted him asylum from what he described as persecution at home.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Hui And Eileen Ng, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Austria's chancellor asked to form new government after parties refuse to work with far-right leader
item.description: Austria’s president has tasked the incumbent chancellor with forming a new government after all other parties refused to work with the leader of the far-right Freedom Party.
item.dc:creator: Philipp Jenne And Geir Moulson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah naval base in Beirut as Iran rallies Gulf Arab nations
item.description: Hezbollah launched missiles on central Israel Tuesday. Meanwhile, Israel Defense Forces said it struck a Hezbollah naval base in Beirut, along with other terrorist targets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tropical Storm Oscar swirls toward the Bahamas after hitting Cuba as a hurricane
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Oscar chugged toward the Bahamas on Tuesday after making landfall in Cuba as a Category 1 hurricane, killing at least six people as it unleashed heavy rains on an island also hit by an unrelated massive power outage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China holds live-fire drills opposite Taiwan week after large-scale exercise
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China is holding live-fire drills off the coast of its southern Fujian province facing Taiwan, just a week after a massive air-and-sea drill it described as punishment for Taiwan's president rejecting Beijing's claims of sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Samoan coast where King Charles will visit worries about the future after ship damaged reef
item.description: SIUMU, Samoa (AP) — Under clear skies Tuesday, shortly after sunrise in the beachside fishing village of Siumu, Samoa, a dozen men and children prepared their small boats for a day at sea.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and AYAKA McGILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken arrives in Israel as US looks to renew cease-fire efforts after the killing of Hamas leader
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken has arrived in Israel on his 11th visit to the region since the outbreak of the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US defense chief promises Ukraine what it needs to fight Russia but goes no further
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The United States “will get Ukraine what it needs” to fight its war with Russia, U.S.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken heads to Middle East in wake of Sinwar's killing
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is going to the Middle East in the wake of the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Israel says more strikes are coming against a Hezbollah-run financial institution
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel said late Monday it planned to carry out more strikes in Lebanon against a Hezbollah-run financial institution that it targeted the night before and which it says uses customers' deposits to finance attacks against Israel.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah generates funds for activities under the guise of a non-profit: Israeli officials
item.description: IDF spokesperson Daniel Hagari revealed that Hezbollah uses a charity under the name Al-Qard Al-Hasan in order to fund terrorist activities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel apologizes for strike that killed 3 Lebanese soldiers
item.description: The Israeli military apologized Monday for a strike that killed three Lebanese soldiers in southern Lebanon, saying it is not battling the country's military and its troops believed they were targeting a vehicle belonging to the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austin warns 'no silver bullet' to defeat Putin as US aid hangs in balance ahead of elections
item.description: Secretary of Defense Austin said there is "no silver bullet" to defeat Russian President Putin and urged the global community to bolster aid to Ukraine as concern mounts over continued U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Public university protests escalate in challenge to Argentina's President Milei
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — In a burst of adrenaline, the Argentine students charged into oncoming traffic. Trucks screeched to a halt. Motorbikes swerved. Some drivers flipped their middle fingers, yelling insults.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cuba fights to come back from a nationwide blackout, then a storm that killed six
item.description: A small town in far eastern Cuba is recovering from flooding that killed at least six people after Hurricane Oscar crossed the island’s eastern coast as a tropical storm with winds and heavy rain.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Rodriguez And Milexsy Duran, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel begins strikes against Lebanon targeting Hezbollah financial assets
item.description: The Israeli Defense Forces began conducting airstrikes in Lebanon on Sunday targeting financial institutions linked to the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba hit by second blackout after grid collapse
item.description: Cubans experienced their second black out wave Saturday after government officials said they were working to restore electrical service to hospitals and crucial service centers after a grid failure on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Strike on Netanyahu home 'a signal' Iran proxies not incapacitated, expert says
item.description: A recent drone strike targeting the home of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is sending "a signal" that Iran-backed militant groups are not "incapacitated," despite losing senior leadership in recent weeks, according to a Middle East expert. Daniel Levy, the president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Funeral home in Poland apologizes after a corpse falls out of a hearse in traffic
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — A funeral home in Poland issued an unusual apology on Saturday after a corpse that it was transporting fell out of a hearse and into traffic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hezbollah fires rocket salvo at northern Israel, Iranian leader vows 'Axis of Resistance' will continue
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces said at least 180 projectiles were fired into Israel from Lebanon as Hezbollah ramps up its attacks on the Jewish state with backing from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harvard donations drop; alums cut ties over anti-Israel protests
item.description: Harvard, the nation's richest university, saw donations plunge 15% after campus protests, new report shows.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, The Hill

item.title: Mexican newspaper offices hit by gunfire in Sinaloa state capital
item.description: A dozen gunshots were fired at a building housing the Mexican newspaper El Debate in the northern state of Sinaloa; the newspaper found at least 4 bullet impacts on the walls of the building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea troops readying for Ukraine combat: South Korea
item.description: South Korea said it's used facial recognition AI tech to identify North Korean officers in eastern Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ju-min Park, NewsNation

item.title: Cuban power plant failure leaves millions in the dark in island-wide outage
item.description: Millions of Cubans were left without power on Friday after a failure at a power plant, the communist government said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia’s war in Ukraine
item.description: South Korea's spy agency said North Korea has sent troops to support Russia's war in Ukraine, transferring 1,500 North Korean special operation forces to the Russian city of Vladivostok.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-Indian government employee charged in US assassination plot
item.description: The Justice Department announced charges against a former Indian government employee in the murder plot against dual U.S.-Canadian citizen Gurpatwant Singh Pannun, a prominent Sikh separatist.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: North Korea sending 10K troops to fight with Russia, Zelensky says
item.description: North Korea is sending around 10,000 troops to Ukraine to fight alongside Russian forces, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Hezbollah vows a new phase in the war, as Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Sinwar
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah vowed to launch a new phase of fighting against invading Israeli troops Friday, a day after Israel said its forces in the Palestinian enclave of Gaza had killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, the chief architect of last year’s brutal attack on Israe
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Israel pressured by UN and US to step up action to tackle Gaza's escalating humanitarian crisis
item.description: Israel’s U.N. Ambassador Danny Danon insisted that his country’s humanitarian efforts remain “as comprehensive as ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy outlines his 'victory plan' to Ukraine's lawmakers, including a call to join NATO
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told lawmakers Wednesday that Ukraine’s Western partners are increasing pressure to negotiate with Russia, but he hinted such talks would be unfavorable to Kyiv as he unveiled what he called his “victory plan” for the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation
